pr.title: ft: Match fn_800D5A30 and fn_800D55B4 in ft_0D31.c
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Attempted to match two previously stubbed fighter functions in src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c and flip ft_0D31.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The implementations were small: fn_800D55B4 synchronizes the fighter's y position upward to another player entity's y position, and fn_800D5A30 calls ftColl_8007B7A4 with p_ftCommonData->x5D8 followed by ft_8008A2BC. The PR was closed unmerged because the batch/worktree had build issues that needed fixing at the source before batching.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder comment for fn_800D55B4 in ft_0D31.c with a C implementation taking Fighter_GObj* gobj.

fn_800D55B4 obtains Fighter* fp from gobj->user_data, gets another player entity via Player_GetEntityAtIndex(fp->player_id, 0), reads its Fighter* user_data, and clamps/raises fp->cur_pos.y to other_fp->cur_pos.y when the other fighter is higher.
Replaced the placeholder comment for fn_800D5A30 with a C implementation that calls ftColl_8007B7A4(gobj, p_ftCommonData->x5D8) and then ft_8008A2BC(gobj).
Changed configure.py so melee/ft/ft_0D31.c was listed as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
The PR body claimed both fn_800D5A30 and fn_800D55B4 were 100% matched, but the PR was closed without merge.
smart_moves: Kept the matched functions minimal and direct, using existing project symbols such as Player_GetEntityAtIndex, ftColl_8007B7A4, ft_8008A2BC, and p_ftCommonData rather than introducing new abstractions.
Used established fighter object access patterns: Fighter_GObj* to gobj->user_data for Fighter*, and HSD_GObj* user_data for another fighter entity.
Updated configure.py to move the whole translation unit to Matching, which is the expected final step once all remaining nonmatching functions in a file are believed matched.
decomp_lessons: Do not flip a file from NonMatching to Matching unless the whole worktree builds cleanly; even small local matches can be blocked by unrelated or batched build failures.
For fighter code, short helper functions may match as simple field reads/writes and calls into already named collision/common helpers; preserving call order is important.
When matching position synchronization logic, compare and assign the specific vector component directly rather than introducing temporaries unless needed for codegen.
Batch-generated matching PRs need source-level validation before submission; this PR was explicitly closed because worktree commits had build issues.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-style names: fn_800D55B4 and fn_800D5A30.
Known common fighter callbacks in this file use ftCo_* naming, e.g. ftCo_Rebirth_IASA, ftCo_RebirthWait_Coll, and ftCo_Rebirth_Cam.
Fighter object variables follow common short names: gobj for Fighter_GObj*, fp for Fighter*, other_gobj for HSD_GObj*, and other_fp for another Fighter*.
Global common fighter data is accessed through p_ftCommonData with field-style offsets such as x5D8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched fn_800D55B4 as a branch-guarded y-coordinate update: only write fp->cur_pos.y when other_fp->cur_pos.y is greater.
Matched fn_800D5A30 as a two-call wrapper with the first call using a global common-data field argument and the second call using only gobj.
The configure.py Matching/NonMatching object switch is the repository mechanism used to mark a translation unit as matching for the build.

review_feedback.feedback: Closing - worktree commits have build issues that need to be fixed at the source before batching.
review_feedback.impact: PR was closed unmerged despite the body claiming both functions matched.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that successfully matched fn_800D55B4 or fn_800D5A30 in ft_0D31.c.
Search for build errors from the claude-batch-20251228-ft0D31 worktree to identify why this batch failed.
Check whether ft_0D31.c was later moved to Object(Matching) in configure.py.
Compare later implementations of fn_800D55B4 and fn_800D5A30 against this closed PR's proposed code.
